questions.promptTex: \text{Sum of interior angles of a hexagon } (n = 6).
\text{Each interior angle of a regular pentagon.}
\text{The exterior angles of any convex polygon always sum to:}
hints: Walking around the polygon, you turn through one full revolution.
How many degrees in a full revolution?
questions.explanation: $360°$. Always — for *any* convex polygon, regardless of $n$. (Walking around the boundary once = a full turn.)

questions.promptTex: \text{Each exterior angle of a regular nonagon } (n = 9).
\text{A regular polygon has each interior angle measuring } 140°. \\ \text{How many sides does it have?}
questions.explanation: Each exterior angle: $180 - 140 = 40°$. Number of sides: $360 / 40 = 9$. So it's a regular **nonagon**.

steps.text: **Solution.** Each exterior angle: $180 - 140 = 40°$. Number of sides: $360 / 40 = 9$. So it's a regular **nonagon**.

questions.promptTex: \text{What is the sum of interior angles of a hexagon (6 sides)?}
\text{The interior angles of a polygon sum to } 1260°. \\ \text{How many sides does it have?}
\text{Find the measure of one interior angle of a regular pentagon.}
questions.explanation: Sum $= 540°$. Each interior angle of a regular polygon = sum / $n$ = $540 / 5 = 108°$.
steps: Sum of interior angles of a pentagon: $(5 - 2) \cdot 180° = 540°$.
Regular polygon → all interior angles equal.

questions.promptTex: \text{Each exterior angle of a regular polygon is } 24°. \\ \text{How many sides does the polygon have?}
hints: Exterior angles of any polygon sum to $360°$.
questions.explanation: Exterior angles sum to $360°$. For a regular polygon, each $= 360/n$. So $360/24 = 15$ sides.
steps: **Key fact:** exterior angles of any convex polygon sum to $360°$ (not $(n-2) \cdot 180$).
Regular polygon → all exterior angles equal.

questions.promptTex: \text{The interior angle of a regular polygon is } 144°. \\ \text{How many sides does it have?}
\text{Three angles of a quadrilateral are } 80°, 95°, \text{ and } 110°. \\ \text{Find the fourth.}
\text{What is the sum of the exterior angles of a regular dodecagon (12 sides)?}
hints: Exterior angle sum is the same for all convex polygons.
questions.explanation: Exterior angles of any convex polygon sum to $360°$, regardless of $n$.
steps: **Counterintuitive but true:** the sum of exterior angles of any convex polygon is **always $360°$**, no matter how many sides.
(Imagine walking around the polygon's boundary. You make a full $360°$ turn — that's the sum.)
